decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest-priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x ratio) will significantly improve lateral heat distribution, lowering the thermal resistance between the chip and the cooled boundaries.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity values may require finer meshing in future iterations to resolve steep gradients accurately, though current mesh should suffice for this step.
